concerns.text: Task T013 mandates 'download/fetch real data... with NO synthetic fallbacks (fail loudly on network error)'. This directly violates the Plan's explicit pivot (Summary) to use simulated DFT data due to the lack of verified experimental datasets. The task forces a failure condition that contradicts the approved implementation strategy, effectively weakening the project's ability to execute the spec's core requirements under the stated constraints.

Task T033 description lists 'sensitivity analysis: 5%, 15%' but omits the 10% threshold. FR-009 and US-3 Acceptance Scenario 2 explicitly mandate a sensitivity analysis over {5%, 10%, 15%}. The task deliverable falls short of the functional requirement it claims to satisfy, constituting a silent relaxation of the spec.

Task T037 describes comparing attention peaks against literature values as a 'sanity check, not pass/fail'. However, SC-003 defines this comparison as a hard success criterion requiring '≥80% of the top 5 attention peaks fall within ±50 cm⁻¹ of literature values'. The task description explicitly removes the enforceability of a defined Success Criterion, violating the constraint that tasks must not silently relax acceptance criteria.

Task T017 specifies 'Scaffold/Template-based splitting'. The Plan (Summary) pivots to 'molecular scaffolds' while the Spec (FR-002) requires 'reaction template (substructures at the reaction center)'. While related, these are distinct chemical concepts. The task description fails to explicitly map the implementation to the specific 'reaction template' requirement, risking a semantic drift where the leakage prevention mechanism does not satisfy the spec's specific constraint.
